data-cloud.name: Data Cloud
data-cloud.description: Data cloud platform with planes architecture

metadata.description: Platform data services and data lake infrastructure provider

nextRequiredWork: Keep Data Cloud registered as a platform provider rather than an ordinary Kernel lifecycle product
Keep bootstrap separation, runtime truth provider, and Action Plane governance evidence current for each release target
Maintain first-class Collection/Dataset/DataSource contracts implementation and backend handlers
Complete connector runtime wiring with real connector provider, runtime truth states, dataset linkage, health, schema, sync, and credential rotation
Maintain typed DAG/node/edge/operator schema and validation for pipeline contracts
Complete audio-video modality as first-class Data Cloud capability with durable MediaArtifact job lifecycle, Transcript, FrameIndex entities, consent, retention, and processing workflow
Complete AEP lifecycle semantics for unified operator model, PatternSpec compiler, EventCloud SPI, pattern lifecycle, learning-to-recommendation loop, and replay-safe agent execution
Context Plane is target-only: planes/context/ directory exists as placeholder and ownership boundary but is not an active Gradle module. UI/runtime should not treat it as fully available.
Keep execution capability optional until runtime truth proves action execution is live in the target profile
Complete UI simplification: hide shell-role switcher in production UX
Do not execute release-readiness or evidence-generation workflows in this implementation hardening pass

gates.description: Bootstrap build must work without Data Cloud providers
All conformance flags must be true with executable proof
First-class Collection/Dataset/DataSource contracts implemented and available in OpenAPI
Connector production path requires real runtime provider, health, schema, sync, credential redaction, and dataset linkage before being marked ready
Audio-video modality requires durable job lifecycle, consent, retention, processor wiring, and result retrieval before being marked first-class

yappc.name: YAPPC
yappc.description: AI-powered code analysis and refactoring platform

conformance.bridgeExemption: Kernel bridge conformance is enabled as YappcProductUnitIntentProvider exports ProductUnitIntent candidates to Kernel. YAPPC uses Data-Cloud evaluation pack infrastructure and Kernel runtime module contracts rather than implementing its own.

metadata.description: Yet Another Platform for Platform Composition — plugin and scaffold framework

nextRequiredWork: Maintain YAPPC lifecycle separation from Kernel product lifecycle
Keep Kernel consumption limited to ProductUnitIntent and artifact-intelligence evidence contracts

gates.description: Creator lifecycle separation from Kernel lifecycle

yappc.productionReadinessReason: YAPPC conformance gates are satisfied. Product is production-ready with full manifest, observability, security, data access, bridge, agent definitions, mastery bindings, evaluation packs, and runtime module conformance.

audio-video.name: Audio-Video
audio-video.description: Audio and video processing services - now integrated as first-class Data Cloud modality

conformance.manifestExemption: Audio-video is integrated as first-class Data Cloud modality via MediaArtifact, MediaProcessingJob, Transcript, FrameIndex, Consent, and RetentionPolicy entities. Product manifest not required as this is a shared service integrated into Data Cloud.

metadata.integrationNote: Audio-video is now a first-class Data Cloud modality with complete entity model and MediaArtifactService workflow

nextRequiredWork: Maintain audio-video as shared service with Data Cloud modality integration
Keep MediaArtifact, MediaProcessingJob, Transcript, FrameIndex entities current with Data Cloud entity model
Maintain MediaArtifactService workflow for upload/register → privacy check → processing job → transcript/frame index → Data Cloud indexing

audio-video.productionReadinessReason: Audio-video is integrated as first-class Data Cloud modality with complete entity model (MediaArtifact, MediaProcessingJob, Transcript, FrameIndex, Consent, RetentionPolicy) and MediaArtifactService workflow. Media privacy, content safety, and artifact retention are implemented through Consent and RetentionPolicy entities. Backend API provides production-grade STT/TTS/Vision services. CI is enabled for build/test/security-scan.
